title: Binary Search Trees
content:
BST is a node-based data structure where each node has at most two children (left and right). The left subtree contains values less than the parent node, and the right subtree contains values greater than the parent node.

Key Operations:
- Search: O(h) where h is height
- Insert: O(h)
- Delete: O(h)

Balanced BSTs (AVL, Red-Black) maintain O(log n) operations.
subject: Data Structures

title: Sorting Algorithms
content:
Comparison-based sorting:
1. Bubble Sort - O(n²)
2. Merge Sort - O(n log n)
3. Quick Sort - O(n log n) average
4. Heap Sort - O(n log n)

Non-comparison based:
1. Counting Sort - O(n+k)
2. Radix Sort - O(d(n+k))

Merge Sort is stable, Quick Sort is not.
subject: Algorithms

title: Newton's Laws
content:
First Law (Law of Inertia): An object at rest stays at rest, and an object in motion stays in motion unless acted upon by an external force.

Second Law: F = ma (Force = mass × acceleration)

Third Law: For every action, there is an equal and opposite reaction.

Applications: projectile motion, friction, circular motion.
subject: Physics
